definitions.gloss_en: to perform or carry out a game action
definitions.gloss_jp: {ゲーム|げーむ}{上|じょう}の{行|こう}{動|どう}を{実|じっ}{際|さい}に{行|おこな}うこと
definitions.explanation_en: Used in rulebooks to instruct a player to carry out a specified action. Implies the action is resolved, not merely declared.
definitions.implicit_notes: 実行する emphasizes concrete execution rather than declaration or intent. In game rules, it marks the resolution step: a player 実行する (carries out) what they have declared.
definitions.orthography_notes: 実行: 実 (actual/real) + 行 (go/carry out). Standard Japanese verb. Weblio dictionary lists it as a general-purpose term used in both everyday and technical (software, game) contexts. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Cross-genre applicable. In wargame rulebooks: '○○を実行する' (execute ○○) is the standard phrasing for rule resolution. Distinct from 宣言する (declare/announce an action).

usage_jp: {行|こう}{動|どう}を{実|じっ}{行|こう}する (execute an action); {突|とつ}{撃|げき}を{実|じっ}{行|こう}する (execute a charge)
comparative_notes: 宣言する (declare) is used when a player announces intent; 実行する is used when the action is actually resolved. Both appear in Japanese wargame rulebooks.

examples.japanese: {彼|かれ}は{計|けい}{画|かく}を{実|じっ}{行|こう}した。

cross_references.note: 攻撃 — a common action that is 実行する'd
